kept.text:
Esto no es solo un conector con Blender. Esto es Anthropic entrando en TODA la industria creativa de un golpe.

Mira la lista completa que han soltado hoy:

→ Blender (modelado 3D)

→ Adobe Creative Cloud (Photoshop, Premiere, Express y 50+ herramientas)

→ Autodesk Fusion (CAD profesional)

→ Ableton Live + Push (producción musical)
→ Splice (samples royalty-free)

→ SketchUp (arquitectura y 3D)

→ Resolume Arena/Wire (visuales en directo y VJ)

→ Affinity by Canva (diseño pro)

Un solo modelo, ahora hablando nativo con prácticamente todo el stack creativo profesional del planeta.

Lo que esto destruye:

→ La fricción entre herramientas (Claude traduce formatos y mantiene assets sincronizados entre 3D, audio y diseño)

→ La curva de aprendizaje (te explica un modifier stack o una técnica de síntesis on-demand)

→ El trabajo repetitivo (batch processing, scaffolding, cambios procedurales en escenas enteras)

→ La barrera entre “tengo una idea” y “está construida” (Claude Code escribe shaders, plugins y scripts directamente para tus herramientas)

El pipeline creativo que conocíamos hace 18 meses ya no existe. La pregunta interesante ahora es qué se vuelve posible que antes no lo era.

kept.text:
Claude just gave one person the production capacity of an entire agency.

Here is how someone will use this to make $1,000,000 in 2026:

Claude now connects directly to Blender, Autodesk Fusion, SketchUp, Adobe Creative Cloud, Ableton, Canva, Affinity, Splice, and Resolume. In Blender, it can debug a scene, build new tools, and batch-apply changes across every object in a project. In Autodesk Fusion, it can create and modify 3D models through conversation. In Ableton, it can automate track routing and control mixing. In Canva and Affinity, it can generate on-brand designs and automate repetitive production steps.

Nine tools. Five creative industries. One subscription.

And if you pair it with SEO Stuff for traditional and AI search optimization? Game over. 

You can check what AI currently says about your creative services using SEO Stuff (it's free):

https://t.co/Pn764BHwyL

Here is what each of those industries currently charges for the work Claude can now help produce.

3D product visualization for e-commerce: a single product render costs $100 to $2,500. A comprehensive product visualization package with multiple angles, lifestyle scenes, and 360-degree spins runs $800 to $3,500. Monthly retainers for brands launching new products regularly run $3,000 to $8,000. There are 5.6 million active Shopify stores and millions of active Amazon sellers worldwide, and Shopify merchants using 3D product content see conversion lifts of up to 94%.

Architectural visualization: a single residential rendering costs $250 to $2,800. Commercial renderings run $450 to $10,000. Animation walkthroughs cost $4,000 to $12,000 per minute. VR production runs $15,000 to $30,000 per project. The architectural design software market is worth $6.7 billion.

Graphic design and branding: a logo costs $300 to $2,500 from a freelancer or agency. A full brand identity package runs $1,000 to $10,000. Monthly design retainers for small businesses run $500 to $6,000. The global graphic design market is worth $59 billion.

Music production: a single produced track costs $200 to $5,000. Mixing runs $300 to $2,000 per song. Sound design charges $25 to $150 an hour. Ableton Live Suite costs $749. Splice runs $20 a month.

Live event visuals: a single-display visual production package runs $3,000 to $7,000. Multi-screen setups run $7,000 to $15,000. LED wall productions run $15,000 to $25,000 or more. Resolume Arena costs around $900.

Every one of these industries is built on the same model: clients pay thousands of dollars because the tools are complex, the learning curve is steep, and the talent is scarce. Claude just collapsed the learning curve.

But here is the part that turns this from a freelance operation into a million-dollar business.

The creative work is the delivery. The thing that determines whether you have five clients or fifty is whether your business is the one that shows up when someone searches for the service. On Google. On ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google AI Overview, and the AI agents that are increasingly finding vendors on behalf of buyers.

Right now, most creative studios have zero AI search presence. A real estate developer in Austin asking ChatGPT for the best architectural visualization studio in Texas gets a generic answer. An e-commerce brand asking Perplexity for the best 3D product rendering service gets a list of marketplaces, not studios. An event producer asking Google AI for a live visuals company in their city gets nothing useful.

Every one of those searches is a client waiting to be won. And the first studio optimized for AI search in each of those verticals, in each major city, locks out everyone who comes after.

Check what AI currently says about your creative services using SEO Stuff (it's free):

https://t.co/Pn764BHwyL

Here is the play.

Step 1: Pick two or three verticals and build production packages around Claude's tool integrations.

The sharpest combination: 3D product visualization for e-commerce brands (Blender and Autodesk Fusion) plus graphic design and branding for the same clients (Adobe Creative Cloud, Canva, Affinity). Every e-commerce brand that needs product renders also needs brand assets, social media graphics, and packaging design. You are solving multiple problems for the same client with the same AI backend.

Build a product visualization package at $2,000 to $4,000 a month: product renders, 360-degree spins, lifestyle scenes, and seasonal refreshes for brands launching new products regularly. Add a brand design retainer at $1,500 to $3,000 a month: social media templates, packaging updates, ad creatives, and brand guideline maintenance.

Your cost to deliver is a Claude subscription, a Blender installation (free), and your creative direction. A traditional studio charges the same prices and employs three to five specialists at $60 to $100 an hour. Your margins are the entire fee minus the cost of your time and judgment.

Architectural visualization is the expansion vertical. Once the e-commerce pipeline is running, add architectural rendering packages for real estate developers and architecture firms at $1,000 to $5,000 per project using Claude's SketchUp and Blender integrations.

Step 2: (This is where the real money is.)

Every client you produce creative work for also needs to be found by their customers. The e-commerce brand that hired you for product renders needs to show up when someone asks ChatGPT for the best version of their product. The real estate developer needs to appear when an AI agent searches for luxury condos in their market. The brand you built a visual identity for needs that identity to show up in AI search results, not a competitor's.

SEO Stuff shows you exactly what AI is saying about each client's business:

https://t.co/Pn764BHwyL

Show them the gaps. Show them the competitors who are showing up instead. Then offer to fix it. Charge $1,500 to $3,000 a month to manage their AI search presence alongside the creative work you are already producing. Recurring revenue. Every month. For every client.

You are now the only studio that makes what you produce actually findable. That is a positioning advantage no traditional agency can match.

Step 3: At 15 clients paying $3,000 a month for creative production and $2,000 a month for AI search management, you are at $75,000 a month. That is $900,000 a year. Add three architectural visualization projects a month at $3,000 each and you are over $1 million. One person with Claude producing the creative work and SEO Stuff driving client acquisition and retention.

https://t.co/eh1auroJF7

Anthropic just connected Claude to the entire creative production stack: 3D modeling, architectural visualization, graphic design, music production, and live event visuals. The tools that used to require five different specialists and five different skill sets now run through a single AI that can operate all of them.

But the tools are just the delivery mechanism. The business is built on being findable. And the creative studios that own the AI search results for their verticals in their cities are the ones that will scale past freelancing into seven figures.

That is the gap SEO Stuff (https://t.co/wKpf0EILTx) was built to close.

Claude builds the creative work. SEO Stuff makes sure the right clients find it. The tools are here. The playbook is above. Someone is going to run this in the next six months and it is going to work.

quoted_tweet.text:
71% of B2B software buyers now rely on ChatGPT, Claude, etc. for software research.

69% of buyers indicated they chose a different software vendor than they initially planned based on AI chatbot convo, and 1/3 purchased from a vendor they had never heard of before.

What does this mean for how your brand actually wins or loses B2B deals in 2026?

That is the question SEO Stuff solves for businesses daily. 

https://t.co/eh1auroJF7 

G2 is the world's largest B2B software marketplace, with over 100 million annual visitors evaluating software products. When G2 surveys 1,076 B2B decision-makers about how they research and buy software, the sample is drawn from the population that actually makes these purchases. 

This particular survey was fielded in March 2026 with respondents across SMB, mid-market, enterprise, and large enterprise segments, spanning individual contributors through VP-level decision-makers. 

(If you want to see how your brand shows up when B2B buyers ask AI chatbots about your category, start here:

https://t.co/Pn764BHwyL)

Here is the finding that changes the sales conversation:

According to G2, 69% of B2B software buyers chose a different vendor than the one they originally planned to buy from, based on AI chatbot guidance. And one-third purchased from a vendor they had never even heard of before.

Think about what that means operationally. 

A buyer enters the research phase with a shortlist in their head. They have a vendor they were leaning toward based on a colleague recommendation, a conference demo, a sales conversation. Then they open ChatGPT or Perplexity or Claude and ask which solution is best for their use case. 

According to G2, 69% of the time the AI answer changes their plan. The AI moves them off the vendor they were going to buy from and onto a different one. And in a third of those cases, the vendor they end up buying from is one they had never even heard of before the AI mentioned it.

This is active deal redirection. The AI is functioning as an unsolicited second opinion that overrides existing preferences in 69% of cases.

(If you want to see how your brand shows up when B2B buyers ask AI chatbots about your category, start here: 

https://t.co/Pn764BHwyL)

According to G2, 51% of B2B software buyers now start their research with AI chatbots more often than with Google. That number was 29% in April 2025. That is a 22-percentage-point shift in 11 months. 

71% of B2B buyers now rely on AI chatbots for software research, up from 60% just seven months earlier. The adoption curve is steepening. The buyers who were still defaulting to Google for research in 2025 are migrating to AI in 2026, and the migration is accelerating.

Here is the trust paradox that creates the commercial opportunity:

64% of B2B buyers encounter inaccurate AI chatbot recommendations often or very often. This is the same population that changed their vendor selection 69% of the time based on AI guidance. 

They know the AI is frequently wrong. They changed their buying decision anyway. What is happening is that the AI is surfacing options the buyer did not previously know about, and the buyer then verifies those options through their own evaluation process: demos, reviews, peer conversations, trial periods. The AI does not close the deal. The AI changes the consideration set. 

And according to G2, it changes the consideration set in 69% of cases despite buyers knowing it is often inaccurate.

The implication for brands is that the accuracy problem does not protect your incumbency. Buyers are aware that AI recommendations are imperfect. They use them anyway. They use them to discover alternatives, to challenge their existing assumptions, and to broaden their evaluation beyond the vendors they already know. If your brand is the one the AI surfaces as an alternative, you get into the evaluation. If your brand is the incumbent the AI replaces with a competitor, you lose the deal you thought you had.

Here is what determines whether AI chatbots recommend your brand or your competitor:

The AI chatbot is assembling its recommendation from the content and authority signals it can access about your category. Expert-attributed content that directly answers the buying questions B2B decision-makers are asking the AI. Third-party editorial coverage from publishers the AI's retrieval system treats as authoritative sources. Review presence on platforms like G2 itself, where the AI pulls comparative data. Domain authority signals that determine whether your content appears in the retrieval set the AI draws from when generating answers.

If your competitor has more of these signals than you do, the AI recommends them. According to G2, that recommendation changes the buyer's plan 69% of the time. The brands that are investing in AI-visible content and authority signals now are the ones getting surfaced as the alternative. The brands that are not investing are the ones being replaced.

This is the system SEO Stuff (https://t.co/wKpf0EILTx) was built around.

Gold Plan

https://t.co/yEFyM0Ze7W

Expert-attributed content backed by DR50+ backlinks designed to make your brand the one AI chatbots recommend when B2B buyers ask the buying question your product answers, targeting the 69% deal-redirection window G2 identified

Premium Content Bundle

https://t.co/4CAnUt07PO

60 pages of expert-attributed content that answers the category and comparison questions B2B buyers are asking AI chatbots, positioning your brand in the consideration set that now determines vendor selection for 51% of software buyers

Premium Backlink Bundle

https://t.co/Z9m9D7TjES

Authority signals from trusted editorial domains that put your brand into the retrieval set AI chatbots draw from when assembling the recommendations that change 69% of B2B buying decisions

The first filter in B2B software buying is no longer Google. It is AI. And the brands that AI recommends are winning deals from competitors who thought those deals were already closed. The question is how many deals you are losing right now because a buyer asked an AI chatbot and the AI said someone else.

kept.text:
🎨 Claude x 크리에이티브 툴과 연결됨

Blender, Autodesk Fusion, Adobe, Ableton, Splice...

방금 Anthropic이 크리에이티브 전문가들을 위한 커넥터 패키지를 공개했네요.

먼저 커넥터는 이래요...🔌

클로드가 외부 소프트웨어에 직접 접근할 수 있게 해주는 연결 도구!

이제 클로드가 툴 안에서 실제로 작업을 수행하거나 코드를 생성하는 방향으로 확장됩니다.

Blender 에서 Python API를 자연어로 조작하고, 씬 분석 / 디버깅 / 오브젝트 일괄 변경 등등등...

Autodesk Fusion 여기에서는 대화로 3D 모델 생성하고 수정도 하구요.

Adobe Creative Cloud, Affinity by Canva.. 되게 많아요!

↓

특히 눈에 띄는건 이건데 Blender와의 관계예요.

Anthropic이 Blender Development Fund의 패트론으로 합류했어요. 오픈소스 개발을 돈으로 후원하는 기업 멤버이라고 보면 됩니다.

단순 커넥터 출시를 넘어 오픈소스 프로젝트에 직접 기여하는 방식..!

그리고 Blender 커넥터는 MCP 기반으로 구축되어, Claude 외 다른 LLM도 사용 가능합니다. 오픈소스 정신과 상호운용성을 지킨 설계입니다.

Claude Code, Cowork, Connectors... 그리고 이번에 크리에이티브!

Anthropic이 수평적 AI 플랫폼에서 산업별 버티컬로 확장하는 패턴이 보이네요???

크리에이티브가 다음 공략 버티컬로 정해진 것인거죠 뭐..ㄷㄷ

↓

정리해보면 클로드가 크리에이티브 작업에서 할 수 있는건..

툴 학습 튜터 / 플러그인이나 스크립트 코드 작성 / 파이프라인 간 포맷 변환 / 반복 작업 자동화 이런 것들이요.

그리고 여기에 새 제품 Claude Design! 소프트웨어 UX 아이디어를 시각화하고, Canva로 익스포트까지.

크리에이티브 AI가 드디어 툴 안으로 들어왔네요. 워크플로우가 바뀌고 있다구요!!

And that’s just the beginning.

Anthropic just launched Claude for Creative Work.

Here’s what Claude can now do:
1. Blender → Debug entire 3D scenes through conversation
2. Autodesk Fusion → Create and modify 3D models just by talking
3. Canva Affinity → Batch-edit layers, rename files, export assets automatically

5. Resolume → Control live visuals in real time with words

You don’t open software anymore.
You just describe what you want.
And it builds it.

This isn’t a plugin.
This isn’t a shortcut.

Claude is now living inside the tools designers spent years mastering.

kept.text:

(Visit Techmeme dot com for the link and full context!)

author.name: andy
author.description: founder mode

kept.text:
in all seriousness i have been one of the longest "let's get ai to benefit CAD" people, way back when it was conceptual and bad, all the way til now where it's getting way better but not there

for people already using autodesk this is great, i hope they use it responsibly and have great review processes with humans in the loop

i still think it's wrong to tell people ai CAD is here and then have them end up on an autodesk sales page for $1600 - fake dream to sell

but i exist to deliver something real

author.name: andy
author.description: founder mode

Claude AI Targets Animation Pipelines, Anthropic Backs Blender

kept.text:
در Autodesk Fusion، قدرتِ تحلیل مهندسی کلود خیره‌کننده‌ست. کلود مدلِ یک کیبورد رو خوند، همه‌ی کلیدها رو دقیق سر جاشون چید و یک نمای انفجاری (Exploded View) بی‌نقص ساخت.
جالب‌تر اینکه کلود خودش تداخل‌ها (Overlap) و فاصله‌گذاری‌ها رو چک و اصلاح کرد. یعنی کلود حالا هندسه و ساختار رو کاملاً درک می‌کنه.

kept.text: Claude will consume the world!

author.name: Matt Workman
author.description:
Creative Technologist @fal
New Media x Gen AI

quoted_tweet.text:
Esto no es solo un conector con Blender. Esto es Anthropic entrando en TODA la industria creativa de un golpe.

Mira la lista completa que han soltado hoy:

→ Blender (modelado 3D)

→ Adobe Creative Cloud (Photoshop, Premiere, Express y 50+ herramientas)

→ Autodesk Fusion (CAD profesional)

→ Ableton Live + Push (producción musical)
→ Splice (samples royalty-free)

→ SketchUp (arquitectura y 3D)

→ Resolume Arena/Wire (visuales en directo y VJ)

→ Affinity by Canva (diseño pro)

Un solo modelo, ahora hablando nativo con prácticamente todo el stack creativo profesional del planeta.

Lo que esto destruye:

→ La fricción entre herramientas (Claude traduce formatos y mantiene assets sincronizados entre 3D, audio y diseño)

→ La curva de aprendizaje (te explica un modifier stack o una técnica de síntesis on-demand)

→ El trabajo repetitivo (batch processing, scaffolding, cambios procedurales en escenas enteras)

→ La barrera entre “tengo una idea” y “está construida” (Claude Code escribe shaders, plugins y scripts directamente para tus herramientas)

El pipeline creativo que conocíamos hace 18 meses ya no existe. La pregunta interesante ahora es qué se vuelve posible que antes no lo era.
